item.title: Patti LaBelle on being called a legend: "I have earned it"
item.description: Patti LaBelle said she's "having fun living it down" as the legendary singer marks her 82nd birthday.

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (May 31)
item.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: Retired Atlanta principal returns to his school, as a handyman
item.description: Last September, David White retired after 33 years in education, the last 15 as principal of the Burgess-Peterson Academy in Atlanta. But retirement didn't sit well with him, and so he applied for a position at his old school, one further down the ladder than his last one. Steve Hartman reports on someone for whom no job – like cleaning gutters – is too small.

item.title: John McWhorter on being "strange," Fats Waller's lost Broadway musical
item.description: John McWhorter talks about his book, "Losing the Race: Self-Sabotage in Black America," published in 2000, along with one of his latest projects.

item.title: Children "held like criminals" inside ICE detention center
item.description: More than 6,300 children under 18 – almost all with no criminal record – have been detained by federal immigration authorities during President Trump's second term, with nearly half held at the Dilley Immigration Processing Center in Texas.

item.title: Children "held like criminals" inside ICE detention center
item.description: More than 6,300 children under 18 – some as young as two months old, and almost all with no criminal record – have been arrested by federal immigration authorities during President Trump's second term. Nearly half have been detained by ICE at the Dilley Immigration Processing Center outside San Antonio. "Sunday Morning" correspondent Jim Axelrod talks with a family that was held at Dilley for almost four months; and with Rep. Joaquin Castro, who is calling for Dilley to be shut down due to inhumane conditions, charges the Trump administration denies.

item.title: Colombians, weary of violence, begin voting in high-stakes election
item.description: A new Colombian president could be elected Sunday, but the election is likely to head to a runoff in June.

item.title: Passage: In memoriam
item.description: "Sunday Morning" remembers some of the notable figures who left us this week, including legendary jazz saxophonist Sonny Rollins.

item.title: Pickleball superstar Anna Leigh Waters
item.description: Professional pickleball is America's fastest-growing sport, and its biggest star is Anna Leigh Waters. Correspondent Jonathan Vigliotti talks with the 19-year-old, whom many call the greatest pickleball player of all time.

item.title: Book excerpt: "View From the East Wing" by Jill Biden
item.description: The former first lady writes of her four years in the White House, her advocacy, and the challenges facing the Biden presidency, from the COVID pandemic and the January 6 insurrection, to the president's health.

item.title: Unveiling the history beneath the Lincoln Memorial
item.description: Beneath the Lincoln Memorial is one of Washington's best-kept secrets: the Undercroft, a soaring 50,000-square-foot foundation built to keep the landmark from sinking into D.C.'s swampy ground. Now home to a museum, the public is being invited to visit underground.

item.title: Unveiling the history beneath the Lincoln Memorial
item.description: For more than a century, one of Washington's best-kept secrets lay beneath the Lincoln Memorial: the Undercroft, a soaring 50,000-square-foot foundation built to keep the landmark from sinking into D.C.'s swampy ground. Beginning in June, the public will be able to visit the space, now with a museum tracing the memorial's history, from its construction to its role as a powerful stage for the civil rights movement. Correspondent Faith Salie goes underground.

item.title: Book excerpt: "Marilyn: The Lost Photographs, The Last Interview"
item.description: To mark the centenary of Marilyn Monroe, her last interview and last formal photo shoot, for Life Magazine writer Richard Meryman and photographer Allan Grant, are now presented in an expanded edition for the first time.

item.title: Almanac: May 31
item.description: "Sunday Morning" looks back at historical events on this date.

item.title: Marilyn Monroe at 100
item.description: She was, and remains, one of cinema's most brilliant stars. Norma Jeane Baker, known to the world as Marilyn Monroe, died in 1962 at age 36, but she left a legacy of classic films, fashion, and a carefully-crafted celebrity image.

item.title: Marilyn Monroe at 100
item.description: She was, and remains, one of cinema's most brilliant stars. Norma Jeane Baker, known to the world as Marilyn Monroe, died in 1962 at age 36, but she left a legacy of classic films, fashion, and a carefully-crafted celebrity image. To mark the centenary of her birth, the Academy Museum of Motion Pictures is launching an exhibition, "Marilyn Monroe: Hollywood Icon." Correspondent Tracy Smith talks with those studying the sex symbol's life and career, and those who are preserving her film persona.

item.title: Special offer for "CBS Sunday Morning" viewers: A free Human Library Card
item.description: Founded 26 years ago, The Human Library offers 30-minute conversations with living books – volunteers who share their knowledge and experience in a wealth of subjects – in 80 countries (including the U.S.) and online.

item.title: 5 Ebola patients have recovered from rare strain, WHO chief says
item.description: The WHO said these five cases exemplify that recovery from the illness is possible, even without approved treatments or vaccines.

item.title: Full Interview: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy
item.description: Watch Margaret Brennan's full interview with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy, a portion of which airs on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on May 31, 2026.

item.title: Full transcript: Cindy McCain on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," May 31, 2026
item.description: The following is the full transcript of the interview with Cindy McCain, executive director of the U.N. World Food Programme, a portion of which aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on May 31, 2026. The interview was taped on May 29, 2026.

item.title: Full transcript: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," May 31, 2026
item.description: The following is the full transcript of the interview with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy, a portion of which aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on May 31, 2026. The interview was taped on May 29, 2026.

item.title: Full Interview: World Food Programme Executive Director Cindy McCain
item.description: Watch Margaret Brennan's full interview with World Food Programme Executive Director Cindy McCain, a portion of which airs on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on May 31, 2026.

item.title: Live Updates: Trump says he's in "no hurry" to make a deal on Iran
item.description: President Trump recently held a meeting with key advisers to hammer out a "final determination" on a potential deal with Iran, but no word has come on a decision.

item.title: Rain threatens search for 2 gold miners still missing in Laos cave
item.description: A Laos rescue organization said​ that the water level inside the cave had receded enough for the four miners to leave with divers.

item.title: Bus driver in deadly Virginia crash charged with involuntary manslaughter
item.description: Jing S. Dong of Staten Island, New York, was charged with two counts of involuntary manslaughter, with additional charges pending, Virginia State Police said.

item.title: White House says Trump is in "excellent health" in results from physical
item.description: President Trump's physician said in a letter released Friday that the president is in "excellent health," following a physical earlier this week at Walter Reed National Military Hospital.

item.title: Protesters, N.J. State Police clash outside Delaney Hall for 2nd night in a row
item.description: Late Saturday, Newark Mayor Ras J. Baraka announced he would be enacting a curfew outside Delaney Hall moving forward.

item.title: Search underway for 4 Alabama inmates who escaped correctional center
item.description: The escaped inmates were being held on various charges, including murder and first-degree robbery.

item.title: Platner's wife told campaign about sexually explicit texts he sent other women
item.description: The wife of Democratic Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner told his campaign in 2025 about sexual messages he had sent to other women.

item.title: Democrats argue ballroom construction shouldn't continue without Congress' consent
item.description: Democratic lawmakers argue the Trump administration must get express consent from Congress before continuing construction on the White House ballroom.
